kept.text:
🚨NEW: We sat down with Andrew McCormick, Head of @eToroUS, to discuss what it takes to operate a crypto exchange across all 50 states.

We cover the patchwork of U.S. regulation, from securing a New York BitLicense to questions around federal preemption and the push for a national standard. Plus, eToro’s IPO, asset vetting, tokenization, and whether prediction markets could arrive on the platform.

Andrew shares why he believes the Clarity Act should move forward, reflects on operating as a public company, and offers perspective on financial literacy and the evolving U.S. crypto market.

quoted_tweet.text:
From our conversations with TradFi brokers, it’s clear that moving to 24/7 is quickly becoming table stakes.

To get there, most are looking at two routes: launching perps (either in-house or via partners) or extending CFDs to 24/7 and hedging the exposure externally.

When discussing the second approach, hedging CFD exposure with perps (instead of futures, leveraged spot, etc.), two structural differences stand out:

1. Deterministic liquidation vs operational margining. In TradFi, margining is rule-based but involves operational processes and potential grace periods, while in perps liquidation is fully deterministic and continuous, ensuring individual account and system-wide solvency without credit assumptions.

2. Managing funding vs rollover / borrow costs. Perp funding is realized more frequently (hourly / 8-hour), and can be significantly more volatile as it reacts in real time to positioning imbalances, compared to the smoother, benchmark-driven cost of carry in futures and financing markets.

None of those are blockers to a wider institutional adoption of perps, but rather observations on how traditional players are already adapting their ways of working to benefit from perps and DeFi.

quoted_tweet.text:
Březen. Měsíc, kdy rozkvétají bledule a kdy nás uhání účetní, aby toho nebylo hodně. Vždycky si vzpomenu na červeného trpaslíka.

„Chovám bláhovou naději, že za 3 miliony let, co jsme pryč, lidstvo přestalo válčit, vyléčilo všechny choroby a podařilo se mu zjednodušit formulář daňového přiznání.“

No zatím to nevypadá. 

Znáte Taxomat? A mohla bych ho vidět? (https://t.co/jqOLnomUEo) a místo lovení denních kurzů ČNB a ručního párování transakcí nechávám appku dělat práci za mě. Umí import z @eToro a dělá to přesně co potřebuju.

Denní kurzy, chytré párování, přehledy... Reálně mi to ušetřilo víc, než stojí předplatné. A ještě zbylo na kafe - a já kafe rád.

A protože se mi líbí, jak to funguje, tak tu mám svůj historicky první afil kód SMUDLA26 a vy máte -20 % na první platbu za libovolné předplatné Taxomatu. 

Nyní nezbývá, než prohlásit "Ale já jsem taky... influencer!" Zatím jeden affiliate, tak to bude 1 rok.

kept.text:
Yesterday, pre-markets opened for Variational and Extended, the two perp DEXs I’m most exposed to.

Most people will look at the token price, and that’s a mistake. The real signal is in FDV and volume.

@variational_io 
• FDV: ~$400M
• Community allocation: ~50%
• Realistic airdrop: ~30%
• Total points: ~7.2M
→ Implied value per point: ~$16–17

@extendedapp 
• FDV: ~$225M
• Airdrop: ~30%
• Total points: ~70M
→ Implied value per point: ~$1

Variational is being priced based on actual volume and OI (top-tier perp DEX already) 

Their upcoming catalysts:
• RWA markets
• API (bots → more volume)

That means current valuations could be conservative if growth continues.

Extended is a different case, more mature product, already proven.

But if the @eToro thesis plays out…
The upside is still there.

Are you going to sit this one out…
or farm both while everyone else is still figuring it out?

Variational:
🔗https://t.co/bSVlOFnzwj
🎟️OMNITOMDEFI

Extended:
🔗https://t.co/xkisvpDtlb
🎟️TOMCRYPTODEFI

tweet.text:
I was so absorbed by the earnings results after the close that I forgot to do an account update, so I will do a quick summary now.

My two main accounts have essentially started moving in opposite directions.

- eToro account is now up 12.47% YTD.
- Trading 212 ISA is now up 6.3% YTD.

I think this discrepancy is largely because $NBIS, by far the largest position in my 212 account, has dropped back from around $160 to $141, and $IREN, another large position, has also dropped from around $53 to $43.

Overall, though, I'm still very happy with these gains, and am confident that both IREN and Nebius will rise again.

$GOOGL rising 6.82% overnight has definitely helped because this is the largest position in my eToro portfolio, and the 2nd largest in my Trading 212 account, however I also have $META in both accounts, which is down 6.82% overnight.
